finding_results.notes: Broke the balanced thesis parallel by separating Priya's accusation (line 167) from her self-confession (line 179), which now arrives as an unexpected pivot rather than a neat 'you did X / I did Y' block. Recovered the snapshot's productive roughness with the 'I could not —' sentence fragment (line 179), consistent with Priya's style-bible stress profile where sentences fragment only under extreme duress. Added Noor's provocative 'It was smaller' (line 181) to create a live exchange that triggers Priya's composure break at 'Do not tell me that' (line 183). Inserted a scene-local trigger (scraping from the lab, line 183) as a physical interruption between speech blocks. Chemo and visa now land concretely ('She is in chemotherapy in Delhi. My visa depends on this position.') but as separate declarations after Priya's composure has already shifted, not as a unified thesis summary. Restored Noor's interrupted line 'The analysis doesn't depend on —' (line 187) from the pre-revision snapshot. Removed the too-neat 'Neither do I' mirror that closed the original exchange. Priya's low contraction level preserved throughout (do not, could not, cannot); Noor's high contraction level maintained (Yeah, doesn't, don't).

summary: Single finding addressed: Priya's corridor confrontation reworked to break the thesis cadence while preserving all must-land facts (falsified citations, chemo in Delhi, visa dependency, credibility collapse). The balanced moral-comparison block was split so Priya's self-confession arrives as an unplanned pivot from her accusation sequence rather than a polished parallel. A scene-local trigger, a sentence fragment under duress, and a provocative exchange between the characters replace the composed position-paper rhythm. Voice separation and contraction levels maintained per style bible.
